source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MOTHERDUCK

Don’t let the quirky name fool you: At MotherDuck, we’re on a mission to make data warehousing fun, frictionless, and ducking awesome for developers and data practitioners building blazing fast internal and customer-facing analytics experiences. We're building a cloud-hosted version of DuckDB, working hand-in-hand with the creators of the fastest-growing and fastest-improving query engine on the planet. Unlike other databases, MotherDuck was architected to unify local development with cloud operations to bridge the gap between software and data engineering workflows. Our team is a mix of thoughtful, passionate, and empathetic data industry veterans.


WHAT YOU WILL DO

MotherDuck is looking for a highly motivated Business Development Representative (BDR) to help drive our growth and introduce companies to the next generation of cloud-based data analytics. This is an opportunity to join our growing BDR team, helping to shape our top of funnel sales strategy and build a strong pipeline of potential customers.

As a BDR at MotherDuck, you’ll be the first point of contact for many of our prospective customers, engaging them in meaningful conversations about how our platform can help them duck and cover from data complexity. You will work closely with our Sales and Marketing teams to identify, engage, and qualify leads, ensuring a seamless handoff to our Account Executives. If you thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and love the challenge of building relationships and identifying business opportunities, this role is for you!

This role based will be located in our New York City office.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Identify & Qualify Opportunities: Listen actively, ask great questions, and determine if a prospect is a strong fit for MotherDuck’s solutions.

 - Pipeline Building: Develop and maintain a pipeline of high-quality leads to support our Account Executives.

 - Product Education: Learn and articulate MotherDuck’s value proposition to prospective customers in a way that resonates with their pain points.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with Sales, Marketing, and Product teams to refine messaging and improve outreach effectiveness.

 - Communicate with Clarity: Explain technical concepts in a way that’s easy to understand, ensuring potential customers grasp the value of MotherDuck.

 - Continuous Improvement: Gather feedback from prospects to help improve our messaging and go-to-market approach.

 - Adapt & Problem-Solve: Tailor your approach based on different industries, customer pain points, and personalities.

 - Own the Process: Maintain accurate records in our CRM, follow up with prospects, and continually refine your outreach strategies.

 - Outbound Prospecting: Conduct targeted outreach via email, phone, and social channels to generate interest and set meetings with potential customers.

 - Stay Curious & Keep Learning: Develop a deep understanding of data analytics, industry trends, and the challenges our customers face.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - A high motor—you thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and love pushing forward.

 - Resilience and adaptability—you see challenges as opportunities and can quickly pivot when needed.

 - Strong communication skills—you know how to engage, listen, and connect with different audiences.

 - Emotional intelligence—you read the room, handle objections with grace, and build trust naturally.

 - Intellectual curiosity— You have a natural curiosity for learning about new industries, technologies, and customer pain points, coupled with a willingness to experiment, iterate, and adapt based on feedback to continuously refine your outreach strategies.

 - A collaborative mindset—you’re a team player who works well across functions and values shared success.

 - Technical aptitude—you’re excited about data and analytics and eager to learn more about how MotherDuck helps companies.

 - A hunter mentality—you’re motivated, persistent, and thrive on find

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MOTHERDUCK

Don’t let the quirky name fool you: At MotherDuck, we’re on a mission to make data warehousing fun, frictionless, and ducking awesome for developers and data practitioners building blazing fast internal and customer-facing analytics experiences. We're building a cloud-hosted version of DuckDB, working hand-in-hand with the creators of the fastest-growing and fastest-improving query engine on the planet. Unlike other databases, MotherDuck was architected to unify local development with cloud operations to bridge the gap between software and data engineering workflows. Our team is a mix of thoughtful, passionate, and empathetic data industry veterans.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

At MotherDuck, we're reimagining cloud-based data analytics with a delightful, developer-first experience powered by DuckDB. We're a team of curious, driven, and thoughtful people who believe simplicity can be powerful, and that selling software should be just as magical as building it.

As we grow, we're looking for an Account Executive who can do more than just run a sales motion, we want someone who helps shape it. The best AEs we've met bring authentic confidence, real urgency, and the kind of entrepreneurial ownership that comes from genuinely believing in what they're selling. If you've spent time in the data analytics, database, or developer tooling space and you light up when a customer finally connects the dots on a hard problem, you might be just the duck we're looking for.

This isn't a role where you'll be handed a polished playbook and told to execute. You'll be selling to data engineers, analysts, and technical decision-makers who will absolutely ask hard questions... and you'll need to love that part of the job. Our deal teams are collaborative by design (AE, customer engineer, support engineer, and manager all working together), so you'll never be going it alone. But you do need to show up with curiosity, grit, and the ability to read the room and adjust appropriately.

This is a hybrid role based out of our San Francisco office (SOMA next to SFPark)





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Drive new revenue: Prospect, engage, and close new business across a mix of inbound and outbound opportunities.

 - Own the full sales cycle: From first touch to signed contract, you’ll guide prospects through the buying process and ensure they feel the value of MotherDuck at every step.

 - Understand the problem space: You’ll connect with data engineers, analysts, and decision-makers to understand their challenges and communicate how we solve them—clearly and convincingly.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Work with product, marketing, and customer success to share feedback, align on messaging, and continuously improve how we go to market.

 - Contribute to the playbook: Help refine sales strategies, positioning, and processes so we can scale efficiently (and delightfully).

 - Build relationships, not just pipeline: Focus on long-term partnerships and helping customers get the most value out of their data.





REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven experience in SaaS sales, ideally within the data analytics, database, or developer tooling space.

 - Strong product and technical curiosity... you don’t need to be an engineer, but you should enjoy learning how the tech works and how it solves real problems.

 - A consistent track record of meeting or exceeding quota and helping build revenue in a fast-moving environment.

 - Drive and resilience... you push through setbacks, learn quickly, and stay focused on the goal.

 - Coach-ability and self-awareness... you seek feedback, act on it, and grow from it.

 - A strong alignment to our values:
   
   - Agency: You take initiative and act like an owner.
   
   - Thoughtfulness: You consider the “why” behind every decision.
   
   - Unorthodoxy: You’re open to creative approaches and fresh thinking.
   
   - Flexibility: You adapt quickly and stay calm in ambiguity.





WHAT WE PROVIDE

 - Feather-ruffling compensati

source_jobs.title: Customer Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MOTHERDUCK

Don’t let the quirky name fool you: At MotherDuck, we’re on a mission to make data warehousing fun, frictionless, and ducking awesome for developers and data practitioners building blazing fast internal and customer-facing analytics experiences. We're building a cloud-hosted version of DuckDB, working hand-in-hand with the creators of the fastest-growing and fastest-improving query engine on the planet. Unlike other databases, MotherDuck was architected to unify local development with cloud operations to bridge the gap between software and data engineering workflows. Our team is a mix of thoughtful, passionate, and empathetic data industry veterans.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

At MotherDuck, we're building a delightful, cloud-native analytics platform powered by DuckDB. We believe in simplifying data without dumbing it down... and we're looking for a Senior Sales Engineer who can help our customers see how magical (and fast!) data can be. This role is perfect for someone who's waddled through the world of data engineering and is now ready to ruffle some feathers in a customer facing role. You'll be the technical tailwind for our sales team, showing prospects how MotherDuck can help them fly through their data challenges with ease.

This is a hybrid role that can be based out of our New York City, Seattle or San Francisco office.


Key responsibilities include

 - Partner with Account Executives to lead the technical side of sales, from discovery through to technical win.

 - Design elegant data architectures using DuckDB, MotherDuck, and the modern data stack... always with simplicity and performance in mind.

 - Create and deliver tailored demos and proof-of-concepts that really get customers flapping with excitement.

 - Translate business needs into scalable, cost-effective, and delightful technical solutions.

 - Work cross-functionally with Product and Engineering to bring customer feedback to the nest and shape the roadmap.

 - Be a trusted technical advisor, helping customers navigate their journey with MotherDuck from pilot to production.

 - Build and deploy AI-powered automations that scale your own impact... think less manual prep, more time wowing customers.






WHAT YOU BRING

 - 5+ years of experience as a data engineer, analytics engineer, or similar, with hands-on skills in SQL, Python, and cloud-native data tooling.

 - 2+ years in a pre-sales or solutions engineering role, ideally in the data or analytics space.

 - Proven, hands-on experience with OLAP workloads... you've designed and optimized for analytical query patterns

 - You've built with or alongside AI systems in a real way... not just using AI tools to go a bit faster, but actually automating workflows, building agents, and multiplying your own output through smart systems.

 - You've worked with tools like BigQuery, Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, and you're always curious about what's hatching in the modern data stack.

 - You’ve worked with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or other agent harnesses to empower yourself and others.

 - You love talking to customers, whiteboarding solutions, and quacking confidently in front of both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Deep familiarity with data warehouse architecture, performance optimization, and data modeling.

 - Experience working in a fast-paced startup environment.

 - [nice to have] Experience with DuckDB and/or MotherDuck (or at least a strong curiosity to learn and dive deep).






WHAT PEOPLE SHOULD BE SAYING IF YOU’RE DOING DUCKING AWESOME

 - "They can land any POC like a pro... our prospects leave every demo flapping their wings in excitement."

 - "They just get the modern data stack and know how to explain it without a 30-slide deck."

 - "They made complex data pipelines feel like a walk in the park... with breadcrumbs."

 - "They're the go-to for any technical deep dive. Calm under pressure, sharp as a beak."

 - "Our customers ask for them by name. Total ducking rockstar."

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Backend
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MOTHERDUCK

Don’t let the quirky name fool you: At MotherDuck, we’re on a mission to make data warehousing fun, frictionless, and ducking awesome for developers and data practitioners building blazing fast internal and customer-facing analytics experiences. We're building a cloud-hosted version of DuckDB, working hand-in-hand with the creators of the fastest-growing and fastest-improving query engine on the planet. Unlike other databases, MotherDuck was architected to unify local development with cloud operations to bridge the gap between software and data engineering workflows. Our team is a mix of thoughtful, passionate, and empathetic data industry veterans.

We’re shaking up assumptions on how data should be done! We empower and delight data practitioners using a novel cloud architecture. We believe in the power of single node databases, built on top of DuckDB, an open source foundation with a vibrant community.

Our engineering team comes from a wide mix of backgrounds, running the gamut from data experts to generalists, from big tech to start ups, and SaaS companies to government and finance.

But no matter where we’ve come from, we’re passionate, collaborative and customer-focused. We work together as a team from across our engineering hubs in Seattle, San Francisco, New York, and Amsterdam.

We’re looking for Backend Engineers to help MotherDuck scale the magic of DuckDB into the cloud. As part of the team, you'll build distributed systems that power query execution, storage, access control, and other core platform capabilities.

If you enjoy systems-level thinking, care deeply about reliability and performance, and want to work on the core of the software that directly improves the experience of analysts and data engineers, this role may be a great fit.



This role can be based in our Seattle, San Francisco or New York office




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Design and implement scalable backend services using Go, Typescript and C++

 - Build and evolve critical systems related to metadata, authentication, access control, query lifecycle, and file visibility

 - Optimize for performance, reliability, and maintainability through careful design and operational rigor

 - Collaborate with other engineers on architectural decisions and technical direction

 - Investigate and resolve production issues and participate in a customer support or production oncall rotation

 - Work closely with product and frontend teams to ensure backend systems enable complete and cohesive user experiences






WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Hands-on experience with distributed systems, preferably using Go, C++, or similar languages

 - A strong understanding of performance, latency, and fault tolerance in real-world production environments

 - Familiarity with API design, observability practices, and production debugging

 - A product-focused mindset and a willingness to dive into unfamiliar areas when needed

 - Ability to communicate technical ideas clearly and collaborate across teams



Example Projects:

 - Implement new database semantics to enable customer usage patterns

 - Improve storage performance by developing and tuning caching layers.

 - Optimize compute provisioning to ensure no noisy neighbors impact customers while keeping infrastructure costs low.






WHAT PEOPLE SHOULD BE SAYING IF YOU’RE DOING DUCKING AWESOME

 - Their code made our queries run faster without anyone asking for it.”

 - “They spotted and fixed a tricky bug before it ever hit production.”

 - “Our backend is easier to understand because of their clear designs and documentation.”

 - “They built a feature that just works… and keeps working.”

 - “They think about the edge cases the rest of us forget.”





WHAT WE PROVIDE

 - Feather-ruffling compensation – competitive salary and stock options so you have a stake in our flock’s success.

 - Top-notch healthcare coverage – 100% paid medical, dental, and vision for employees, plus 80% coverage for dependents (b

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MOTHERDUCK

Don’t let the quirky name fool you: At MotherDuck, we’re on a mission to make data warehousing fun, frictionless, and ducking awesome for developers and data practitioners building blazing fast internal and customer-facing analytics experiences. We're building a cloud-hosted version of DuckDB, working hand-in-hand with the creators of the fastest-growing and fastest-improving query engine on the planet. Unlike other databases, MotherDuck was architected to unify local development with cloud operations to bridge the gap between software and data engineering workflows. Our team is a mix of thoughtful, passionate, and empathetic data industry veterans.

We're shaking up assumptions on how data should be done! We empower and delight data practitioners using a novel cloud architecture. We believe in the power of single node databases, built on top of DuckDB, an open source foundation with a vibrant community.

Our engineering team comes from a wide mix of backgrounds... running the gamut from data experts to generalists, from big tech to startups, and SaaS companies to government and finance. But no matter where we've come from, we're passionate, collaborative and customer-focused. We work together as a team from across our engineering hubs in Seattle, San Francisco, New York, and Amsterdam.

MotherDuck's infrastructure team builds the systems and tools that make our platform reliable, secure, and easy to operate. We run our platform on Kubernetes, so if you're someone who genuinely enjoys living in K8s... debugging cluster issues, tuning autoscaling, thinking through multi-tenancy and workload isolation... this is a team built for you.

If you're energized by a blend of cloud systems work, software development, and operational strategy, we'd love to hear from you.

This role is based in Amsterdam office.





WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own and evolve our Kubernetes platform, including cluster architecture, upgrades, multi-tenancy, and workload isolation

 - Build and maintain infrastructure components using Go and Python across compute, networking, and storage

 - Design and improve CI/CD pipelines that enable safe and fast software delivery

 - Implement observability tooling and dashboards that surface system health and improve oncall effectiveness

 - Automate operational workflows, especially around K8s operations, to reduce manual intervention and increase consistency

 - Collaborate with product and backend teams to embed operational best practices into product development

 - Participate in incident response and ongoing efforts to improve system reliability and security


Example Projects

 - Autoscaling our Kubernetes clusters in the EU region and building out private link networking

 - Designing secure CI/CD pipelines with observability baked in from the start

 - Building logging frameworks that make cross-system debugging way less painful




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Hands-on Kubernetes experience... you've operated clusters in production, not just deployed workloads onto them

 - Experience working with cloud-native infrastructure more broadly (AWS, Terraform, service meshes, etc.)

 - Programming skills in Go and/or Python, and the ability to design systems that are secure, scalable, and maintainable

 - Familiarity with observability tooling such as Prometheus, Grafana, or OpenTelemetry

 - A real sense of ownership and a pragmatic approach to engineering tradeoffs

 - Interest in building internal tools and automation to make development and operations easier for others




WHAT PEOPLE SHOULD BE SAYING IF YOU'RE DOING DUCKING AWESOME

 - "Their automation saved me hours this week, and I didn't even have to ask."

 - "Our oncall pages are quieter because of the systems they built."

 - "They made our CI/CD pipeline so smooth I almost miss the old chaos... almost."

 - "Every dashboard they touch actually tells me something useful."

 - "Our K8s clusters just work, and when they don't, they're already t
